capability: structural-reveal discrimination — isolate a line whose bond joins two previously-separate regions of the entity graph
claim: A bond between two STANDING entities sitting in separate graph components is a structural reveal; the interior connectivity channel (bridgeSurprise) fires maximally on it and ~0 on surface-matched controls (adjacent re-bond, fresh entity), while the mass-based one-surprise (bayesBits) does NOT isolate it (ranks fresh-mass lines above it); the connectivity reading is invariant to a surface emphasis cue.
stimulus_shape: text · discrimination+invariance · L3 · whole-reading · target CON
verdict: CONFIRMED — controls dark (adj=0, fresh=0), bridge unique-argmax on the reveal (1.0), bayes argmax elsewhere (gap real), surface-invariant (unmarked==marked)
mechanism: connectivity collapse over the CON/SIG bond graph: a bond's endpoints in different components -> 1, same-component geodesic delta -> (delta-1)/Dinf, adjacent -> 0; endpoints resolved through the CAUSAL SYN-merge identity quotient (the engine's own union-find, restricted to sentIdx<=cursor) so a reveal that names a standing entity by a new surface form is seen
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new src/core/bridge.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; wired into src/perceiver/reading.js as an OPT-IN channel readingAt({bridge:true}) so the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: the mass one-surprise (surprise.js) has no atom for connectivity — a bond among standing entities is a tiny mass deposit; the structural convergence leaves no trace in the figure field. The existing diagnostic (scripts/reveal-discrimination.mjs bridgeSurprise) located this but lived only in the read-only harness AND keyed on raw surface ids, so it MISSED short-name reveals and faked a 2-hop separation on adjacent controls.
parity: npm test 649->658 (9-test lock added), default readingAt byte-identical (bridge opt-in)
generalization: 194/194 lines of the original reveal-stimulus.json reproduce the prior raw-id bridge column EXACTLY (no regression); coref-resolution is a strict superset — identical where reveals use full names, strictly better on short-name reveals
omnimodal: CONFIRMED on a pure-operator log (no text/parser): cross-component bond -> 1.0, adjacent -> 0, SYN-coref reveal -> 1.0 — the mechanism reads only operators, so any organ emitting CON/SIG/SYN inherits it
scope_holds: any modality whose organ emits bonds (CON/SIG) and identity merges (SYN); structural reveals that join graph regions; reveals naming a standing entity by a new surface form
scope_fails: a reveal carried by NO bond (a pure predication/contradiction leaves connectivity unchanged — the enacted significance loop's province); convergence the weak SVO parser does not draw as a bond or shared object (front-end limit, not the channel's)

capability: signal-derived novelty reserve — the mass the reader holds for an as-yet-unseen atom tracks the recent NOVELTY RATE, not a hand-rolled constant
claim: the reserve amplitude is the γ-decayed mass of recent newcomer admissions (same γ as the figure field); run through the SAME fixed Born step it makes the reserve share nu/(total+nu) track how often newcomers have been arriving, so the same newcomer is half-expected after a churn and a violation after a sealed run — where the constant 1/(total+1) reads them identically; a count-matched control (stale-burst vs fresh-churn, identical counts) isolates recency from count
stimulus_shape: text + pure-operator/tonal · discrimination+invariance · L3 significance · across-a-window · target the reserve/protention (NOVELTY_RESERVE)
verdict: CONFIRMED — default FLAT (reserveConst 0.231/0.231/0.231, bayes 0.29/0.29/0.29 across sealed, stale-burst, fresh-churn), signal-derived reserve dissociates (0.002 / 0.054 / 0.493), count-matched gap ~9x on identical counts, newcomer significance 2.29 (sealed) vs 0.08 (churn) where the default reads both 0.29
mechanism: the reserved amplitude tracks the signal's own recent history: each newcomer admission deposits γ^(reading-distance) — the same w the figure field lays down — and the reserve is their sum (noveltyAmplitude); context enters at the amplitude, the fixed Born step (reserve=amp/(Σ+amp)) is unchanged; cold start falls back to the seed, where the KL is identically zero for any positive reserve
fix_layer: interior (genome) — new noveltyAmplitude in src/core/surprise.js, exported from src/core/index.js beside surpriseAt; threaded into src/perceiver/reading.js as an OPT-IN (readingAt({adaptiveReserve:true})) so surpriseAt, forwardDist, the surprisal pNovel and the new reserve/noveltyMass/totalMass channels use it only when asked — the default path is byte-identical
root_cause_of_gap: NOVELTY_RESERVE=1.0 is a hand-rolled constant in the predictive path; the reserve share 1/(total+1) is a function of total mass alone, with no term for the recent newcomer rate — the reader cannot learn from its own novelty signal
parity: npm test 676->685 (9-test lock added); default readingAt byte-identical (adaptiveReserve opt-in); exp-0001 structural-reveal re-run reproduces its bridge AND bayes columns exactly (data/structural-reveal-out.jsonl unchanged)
generalization: raises competence (a capability the engine lacked) and breaks none; OMNIMODAL — the same readingAt path on a parser-free TONAL operator log dissociates churn from a repeated motif (reserve 0.500 vs 0.018, newcomer bayes 0.07 vs 1.36) while the constant reserve reads both 0.233; the interior reads only operators
omnimodal: CONFIRMED in two senses (text figures + tonal pitches via a pure-operator log) — the mechanism reads only INS admissions and the γ kernel, so any organ emitting newcomers inherits it
scope_holds: any modality whose organ admits newcomers (INS) onto the log; the significance channel (surpriseAt KL), the forward distribution (forwardDist p(next)) and the surprisal reserve when adaptiveReserve is on
scope_fails: the DEFAULT scoring path is unchanged (the fix is opt-in pending a RULES_REV golden-parity swap, the same discipline appearance-time and Track-A hold); newcomer detection keys on the raw id (the appearance-time residue), so a referent re-entering under a NEW surface form still counts as a newcomer until coref pooling ships
